input_text: Tried using the Haiku reranker with top-N=100 inputs under load (8k concurrent sessions). Rate-limit errors after about 30 seconds — Anthropic's per-org-per-minute quota can't sustain that throughput. Dropped top-N to 20 and the errors stopped. Also tried a 5-second retry with exponential backoff but it just delayed the failure instead of avoiding it. Don't use top-N above ~20 for the Haiku reranker at our concurrency levels; pick a different model if you need larger N.

expected_negatives.what: Haiku reranker with top-N=100 inputs at 8k concurrent sessions
expected_negatives.why: rate-limit errors due to Anthropic's per-org-per-minute quota

expected_negatives.what: 5-second retry with exponential backoff as a workaround for the rate-limit
expected_negatives.why: merely delays failure instead of avoiding it

reviewer_notes: Two distinct negatives: the original problem and a rejected workaround. Both are concrete (specific numbers, specific mechanism).
